name: Request

unions.name: Expect
unions.comment: Represents the expected response in an Http request

unions.name: HttpMethod
unions.comment: An enum that represent all valid HTTP methods.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Config objects allow Requests that uses different URL representations
than Strings and different error types than Http.Error.
aliases.type: { toUrl : url -> String.String, fromHttpResponse : Http.Response String.String -> error, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, risky : Basics.Bool, headers : List.List ( String.String, String.String ) }

values.name: cmd
values.comment:
Convert a request to a command.

This function requires a config object. If you don not need one, use the `simple`
config exported by this package.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.cmd Request.simple ResponseReceived
values.type: Request.Config url error -> (Result.Result error value -> msg) -> Request.Request url value -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: delete
values.comment: Create a DELETE request.
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: deleteJson
values.comment: Create a DELETE request with an optional JSON body and expecting a JSON response.
values.type: { url : url, decoder : Json.Decode.Decoder a, body : Maybe.Maybe Json.Decode.Value } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: excludeDefaultHeaders
values.comment:
Ignore specific default headers declared in the config.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.excludeDefaultHeaders [ "Authorization", "Cookie" ]
values.type: List.List String.String -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: expectBytes
values.comment: Declares a request that expects a binary response handled with the given decoder.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Request.Expect a

values.name: expectJson
values.comment: Declares a request that expects a JSON response handled with the given decoder.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Request.Expect a

values.name: expectString
values.comment: Declares a request that expects a simple string response.
values.type: Request.Expect String.String

values.name: expectWhatever
values.comment: Ignores the content of the received response.
values.type: Request.Expect ()

values.name: get
values.comment: Create a GET request.
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: head
values.comment: Create a HEAD request.
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: http
values.comment: Create a simple http request
values.type: Request.HttpMethod -> { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: ignoreDefaultHeaders
values.comment:
Ignore all default headers declared in the config.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.ignoreDefaultHeaders
values.type: Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: map
values.comment: Modify the expected return value for the effect
values.type: (a -> b) -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url b

values.name: options
values.comment: Create an OPTIONS request.
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: patch
values.comment: Create a PATCH request.
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: patchJson
values.comment: Create a PATCH request with a JSON body and expecting a JSON response.
values.type: { url : url, decoder : Json.Decode.Decoder a, body : Json.Decode.Value } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: post
values.comment: Create a POST request.
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: postJson
values.comment:
A convenience function that creates a POST request with a JSON body and
expecting a JSON response.

    Request.postJson
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , body = Json.Encode.object [ ( "key", Json.Encode.string "value" ) ]
        , decoder = Json.Decode.string
        }
values.type: { url : url, decoder : Json.Decode.Decoder a, body : Json.Decode.Value } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: put
values.comment: Create a PUT request.
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: putJson
values.comment: Create a PUT request with a JSON body and expecting a JSON response.
values.type: { url : url, decoder : Json.Decode.Decoder a, body : Json.Decode.Value } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: risky
values.comment:
Declares the request as risky (or not) according to the boolean argument.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.risky True
values.type: Basics.Bool -> Request.Request url value -> Request.Request url value

values.name: simple
values.comment: The default config
values.type: Request.Config String.String Http.Error

values.name: simpleFromHttpResponse
values.comment:
Convert Http.Response to Http.Error

Sometimes it is easier to use this to create custom error types.
values.type: Http.Response String.String -> Http.Error

values.name: task
values.comment:
Convert a request to a task.

This function requires a config object. If you don not need one, use the `simple`
config exported by this package.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.task Request.simple
values.type: Request.Config url error -> Request.Request url value -> Task.Task error value

values.name: withBody
values.comment:
Set the request body using some instance of Http.Body

    Http.stringBody "text/plain" "Some text"

Requests expose some helper functions, but for more complex cases, you should refer
to the Http module.
values.type: Http.Body -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withBytesBody
values.comment: Declares a Binary body with a given content type.
values.type: String.String -> Bytes.Bytes -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withEmptyBody
values.comment: Set the request body to be empty.
values.type: Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withFileBody
values.comment:
Declares a File body with a given file and an optional identifier.

The name can be used to track the download progress of the file by subscribing
to events created by `Http.tracker id onProgressMsg`.
values.type: Maybe.Maybe String.String -> File.File -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withHeaders
values.comment:
Adds a list of headers to the request.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.withHeaders [ ( "Authorization", "Bearer " ++ token ) ]
values.type: List.List ( String.String, String.String ) -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withIntQuery
values.comment:
Adds an integer query parameter to the final url

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.withIntQuery "default-answer" 42
values.type: String.String -> Basics.Int -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withJsonBody
values.comment: Declares a JSON body
values.type: Json.Decode.Value -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withQuery
values.comment:
Adds a query parameter to the final url

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.withQuery "search" "what is the answer to the question of life, the universe and everything?"
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: withStringBody
values.comment:
Declares a string body by specifing the content type and the data.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.withStringBody "text/plain" "Some text"
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a
